name: Green Beans in Black Bean Sauce
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp low sodium soy sauce or tamari
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash and trim the green beans into bite-sized pieces.
2. Steam or microwave the green beans with a little water until just tender-crisp. Drain and rinse with cold water to stop cooking.
3. Mix together the black bean paste, soy sauce, water, and corn starch in a bowl until very smooth.
4. Heat a wok or large saucepan over medium-high heat. Add some water and the cooked green beans.
5. When the beans are heated (about 2 minutes), add the sauce mixture. Stir constantly to coat the beans. The sauce will thicken as it cooks.
6. Serve immediately over hot cooked rice.
This sauce and cooking process can be used with various vegetables, such as asparagus, which is a popular choice for this recipe.
